title: Bubble Up for Baking Soda Day!
story: Every December 30, people celebrate the amazing white powder sitting in their kitchen cabinets! National Bicarbonate of Soda Day honors baking soda, the superhero ingredient that makes cookies fluffy, cleans stains, and creates awesome volcano experiments. Families bake special treats, try fun science projects that fizz and bubble, and share cool baking soda tricks on social media. Some people even make homemade toothpaste or deodorizer to show off this wonder powder's many talents!
activity_question: According to the story, what are some ways people use baking soda on this holiday?
activity_choices: Baking treats and doing science experiments
activity_answer: Baking treats and doing science experiments
follow_up_question: What other surprising uses for baking soda can you think of around your house?

title: Sizzling Bacon Day Brings the Crunch
story: Every December 30, bacon lovers across America celebrate one of the most delicious holidays ever created! On Bacon Day, people wake up early to fry crispy strips for breakfast, share bacon recipes online, and even wrap other foods in bacon just for fun. Restaurants offer special bacon burgers, bacon pizza, and bacon ice cream that sounds weird but tastes amazing. Social media fills with sizzling photos and bacon jokes all day long. Some families have bacon cook-offs to see who makes the crunchiest strips, while others try crazy bacon experiments like bacon cookies or bacon-wrapped everything. It's the perfect day to celebrate America's favorite breakfast meat!
activity_question: What do some families do on Bacon Day to see who cooks the best?
bonus_fact: Americans eat about 18 pounds of bacon per person every year!
